title: Didiscus novoguinensis
taxonomy: Didiscus novoguinensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Didiscus, which is a small group of plants native to New Guinea. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Didiscus novoguinensis is one of the lesser-known species within the family Asteraceae. The classification of Didiscus novoguinensis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.

intro: Didiscus novoguinensis, also known as the New Guinea didiscus, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Didiscus novoguinensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
